source: derived from data/history team strings + documented lineage
attribution: Team name strings and year ranges enumerated from data/history/1967.json through data/history/2026.json, which derive from the f1db dataset (CC BY 4.0) - https://github.com/f1db/f1db. Lineage relationships and notes are authored from widely documented, uncontroversial motorsport history; no statistics are asserted beyond the year ranges observed in the data files.
aliasPolicy: An alias list groups strings only when they are trivially the same constructor with sponsor or engine-suffix variations. No such variant strings occur in the current data, so every alias list contains exactly the canonical string. Historically connected but distinct team identities are kept separate, with the connection recorded as an explicit lineage relationship rather than a silent merge. Records whose single name string is known to conflate more than one historical entity are marked isComplete false with an explanatory note.

teams.note: This name string conflates two entities: the Alfa Romeo works team of 1979-1985 and the Sauber-operated entry that raced under the Alfa Romeo name in 2019-2023.

lineage.note: The 2019-2023 Alfa Romeo entries are the Sauber team racing under a branding agreement; the team reverted to a Sauber identity for 2024.

sources: documented F1 constructor lineage (uncontroversial general motorsport history)
